text.title: Dashboard Agent Pipeline and Gotchas
text.summary: Multi-tool reporting agent pipeline, subagent patterns, Python reporting script, and cross-tool metric gotchas.

rule.statement: Separate dashboard setup (one-time configuration) from report generation (recurring run); agents must be able to perform both independently without conflating them.
rule.rationale: Mixing setup and reporting logic means a broken report run can corrupt the dashboard configuration; keeping them separate makes each independently testable.

rule.statement: Validate data completeness at pipeline start: if any required metric returns null or zero unexpectedly, send a data quality alert to the ops channel rather than delivering a misleading report.
rule.rationale: A sprint with 0 committed items is almost certainly a JQL error, not reality; false zeroes cause stakeholder responses to non-existent problems.

rule.statement: Cross-tool joins (Jira sprint + GitHub deploys + PagerDuty incidents) require a shared key (issue number or release tag) present in all systems — if the key is missing, joins silently drop rows.
rule.rationale: Silent row drops produce metrics that appear correct but are systematically undercounted; always validate join coverage before delivering cross-tool reports.

section.title: Python Reporting Script
section.p: Minimal pattern: query Jira, validate results, compute status, post to Slack.
section.reference: Prompt for agent to compute and render a weekly status report

example.code:
import os, requests from datetime import date
JIRA_URL = os.environ["JIRA_URL"] JIRA_TOKEN = os.environ["JIRA_TOKEN"] SLACK_HOOK = os.environ["SLACK_WEBHOOK"] PROJECT = os.environ["JIRA_PROJECT"]
def jql_count(jql): r = requests.get( f"{JIRA_URL}/rest/api/3/search", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {JIRA_TOKEN}"}, params={"jql": jql, "maxResults": 0}, ) data = r.json() if "total" not in data: raise ValueError(f"JQL failed: {data}") return data["total"]
sprint = f"project = {PROJECT} AND sprint in openSprints()" total = jql_count(sprint) done = jql_count(f"{sprint} AND status = Done") blocked = jql_count(f"{sprint} AND labels = blocked AND status != Done")
if total == 0: requests.post(SLACK_HOOK, json={"text": "WARNING: Sprint query returned 0 items — check data quality."}) else: pct = round(done / total * 100) status = "On Track" if pct >= 85 else ("At Risk" if pct >= 70 else "Off Track") msg = ( f"*Sprint Status ({date.today()}): {status}*\n" f"Committed: {total} | Done: {done} ({pct}%) | Blocked: {blocked}" ) requests.post(SLACK_HOOK, json={"text": msg})
example.note: Raises ValueError on bad JQL response; handles zero-total edge case with explicit data quality alert; computes On Track/At Risk/Off Track threshold.

list.item: dashboard-builder-agent: given PM tool config and metric spec, scaffolds dashboard widget layout and outputs setup checklist.
multi-tool-reporter: queries Jira + Linear + GitHub on schedule, merges metrics into a unified status table, posts to Slack, archives report.
etl-pm-agent: nightly; fetches incremental PM data, validates schema, upserts to Postgres, triggers Metabase/Grafana dataset refresh.
